evidence.text: Demand for our hardware and software products and services increased during the three month period ended April 2, 2021.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates accelerating demand, supported by evidence of increased sales.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices.

evidence.text: Price increases are reflected as a component of the change in sales from existing businesses, and year-over-year price increases contributed 1.0% to sales growth during the three months ended April 2, 2021 versus the comparable period in 2020.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successfully raising prices contributes to sales growth, indicating a positive trend.

evidence.text: The year-over-year increase in gross profit during the three months ended April 2, 2021, as compared to the comparable period in 2020, is primarily due to a favorable sales mix which was partially offset by increased costs of materials due to inflationary pressures.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: A favorable sales mix, despite cost increases, suggests a positive revenue trajectory.

evidence.text: Sales from existing businesses increased 14.3% during the three months ended April 2, 2021, as compared to the comparable period in 2020.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: 14.3% growth in sales from existing businesses shows strong organic growth.

evidence.text: Higher year-over-year sales volumes, price increases and a favorable sales mix — favorable 700 basis points

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Higher year-over-year sales volumes indicate growing unit demand.

factors.summary: End markets are growing fastest in high-growth markets.

evidence.text: High growth markets had a more than 20% increase primarily due to growth in India and Latin America.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Over 20% growth in high-growth markets highlights significant positive momentum.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to inflation.

evidence.text: Cost of sales increased $49.5 million for the three months ended April 2, 2021, as compared to the comparable period in 2020, due primarily to higher year-over-year sales volumes from existing businesses as well as increased costs of materials due to inflationary pressures.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising input costs due to inflation pose a challenge, impacting margins negatively.

evidence.text: The 90 basis point increase in gross profit margin during the three months ended April 2, 2021 as compared to the comparable period in 2020 is primarily due to a favorable sales mix.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement in gross profit margin due to favorable sales mix indicates positive margin expansion.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes are creating costs and risks.

evidence.text: Changes in, or status of implementation of, industry standards and governmental regulations, including interpretation or enforcement thereof, may reduce demand for our products or services, increase our expenses or otherwise adversely impact our business model.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The evidence indicates that regulatory changes are causing costs and risks, which negatively impacts the business.

factors.summary: The company faces material litigation risks.

evidence.text: We are party to asbestos-related product litigation that could adversely affect our financial condition, results of operations and cash flows.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces material litigation risks that could significantly affect its financial condition.

factors.summary: Management expects continued growth but remains cautious.

evidence.text: We expect overall sales and sales from existing businesses to grow on a year-over-year basis in 2021; however, we are continuing to monitor the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic and geopolitical and regulatory uncertainties and the corresponding impact to our businesses in addition to the other factors identified above in 'Information Relating to Forward- Looking Statements'.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management expects growth but remains cautious, indicating a positive outlook despite external uncertainties.

factors.summary: Management is cautious due to external risks.

evidence.text: Forward-looking statements speak only as of the date of the report, document, press release, webcast, call, materials or other communication in which they are made. We do not assume any obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statement, whether as a result of new information, future events and developments or otherwise.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management's cautious approach suggests a lack of confidence due to external risks.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include geopolitical and regulatory uncertainties.

evidence.text: Important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those envisaged in the forward-looking statements include the following: • The effect of the COVID-19 pandemic on our global operations and on the operations of our customers, suppliers, and vendors is having, and is expected to continue to have, a significant impact on our business and results of operations.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include significant geopolitical and regulatory uncertainties impacting operations.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include commodity prices and foreign exchange rates.

evidence.text: Our financial results are subject to fluctuations in the cost and availability of commodities that we use in our operations. Changes in foreign currency exchange rates positively impacted our sales growth by 1.8% during the three months ended April 2, 2021 compared to the comparable period in 2020.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals from foreign exchange positively impacting sales but commodity prices posing a risk.

evidence.text: High growth markets had a more than 20% increase primarily due to growth in India and Latin America.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: High growth markets indicate favorable economic conditions as a tailwind.

evidence.text: Changes in foreign currency exchange rates positively impacted our sales growth by 1.8% during the three months ended April 2, 2021 compared to the comparable period in 2020.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements have positively impacted sales growth, indicating a very positive development.

evidence.text: International economic, political, legal, compliance, epidemic and business factors could negatively affect our financial statements.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Evidence indicates that geopolitical factors could negatively impact financial statements.

evidence.text: As of the date of this quarterly report, we have outstanding indebtedness of approximately $2.0 billion and the ability to incur an additional $750.0 million of indebtedness under the Revolving Credit Facility, as defined above, and in the future we may incur additional indebtedness, all of which could adversely affect our businesses and our ability to meet our obligations and pay dividends.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: High debt levels pose a moderate risk to the company's financial health.
